findings.evidence: DerivativeTest.lean:403 `isLocalMin_of_deriv_deriv_pos (hf ...) (hd ...) (hc : ContinuousAt f x₀)`; LocalExtr/Basic.lean:251 `IsLocalMax.deriv_eq_zero` has no differentiability hypothesis. `hg` appears only as `hg.continuousAt` in the proof.
findings.fix: Weaken to `(hg : ContinuousAt g t₀)` and replace `hg.continuousAt` with `hg` in the proof.
findings.issue: `deriv_deriv_nonpos_of_isLocalMax` assumes `hg : ContDiffAt ℝ 2 g t₀` but the proof uses `hg` only as `hg.continuousAt`; `hmax.deriv_eq_zero` needs no differentiability and `isLocalMin_of_deriv_deriv_pos` requires only `ContinuousAt`. The assumption is stronger than needed and than the Mathlib convention.

findings.evidence: Derived from `deriv_deriv_nonpos_of_isLocalMax (g := -g) hg.neg hmin.neg`, which per the fix above needs only `ContinuousAt`.
findings.fix: Weaken to `(hg : ContinuousAt g t₀)`; `hg.neg` remains valid via `ContinuousAt.neg`.
findings.issue: `deriv_deriv_nonneg_of_isLocalMin` inherits the same over-strong `ContDiffAt ℝ 2` hypothesis via `hg.neg`; only continuity is needed.

summary: The 1D necessary second-derivative tests assume `ContDiffAt ℝ 2` but use only `ContinuousAt`; the second-order strength is unused and weaker than Mathlib's sibling lemma `isLocalMin_of_deriv_deriv_pos` (which needs only `ContinuousAt`). The multivariate results are at the natural level.
